customer_anxiety_profile: Low anxiety, high warmth. Carter is a recycled lead who returned the missed call and volunteered three available days. His closing energy ('K. Awesome. Alright.') is genuine. The risk is structural (unverified credit qualifier), not relational.
unresolved_concerns: Credit score not verified — Carter said he would check 'when I get home' but no confirmation mechanism was locked in. Prior DQ on credit is the known failure point from the earlier meeting.
Appointment is explicitly conditional ('if the credit is good') with no fallback path defined if credit is borderline or still below threshold.
sensitive_topics_raised: Prior credit disqualification — Carter was previously screened out on credit. Closer should not re-raise this as a surprise; Carter knows it's a factor.
exact_promises_rep_made: Francisco promised to text Carter with his phone number and appointment details.
Appointment booked: Monday at 2PM, conditional on credit score above 670.
Francisco described the appointment as 'about thirty minutes' and framed it as an in-person visit ('we just come by').
closer_should_know: Carter is a warm lead — he called back, volunteered availability, and closed enthusiastically. The only structural risk is the unverified credit qualifier from a prior DQ. Confirm credit status before the appointment; if Carter never replied to Francisco's text, a brief outreach to confirm the slot (and the credit check) is warranted before Monday at 2PM. Do not treat this as a cold or reluctant lead — the relationship is solid.
